example_scripts.script:
The river doesn't ask the stone
if it's ready to be moved.

It just… keeps going.
Patient. Persistent. Unbothered.

And the stone—
worn smooth over years of resistance—
doesn't call that loss.

It calls it shape.

The things that weathered you
weren't trying to destroy you.
They were finding
what was underneath.

From the outside, the house looked warm.
Lights on. Curtains drawn.

But windows don't lie
the way faces do.

They show you what someone can't say—
the empty chair at dinner,
the mug sitting cold on the counter,
the long exhale before someone smiles.

A person can fill their life with everything
and still live in it alone.

Share this with someone
who would never tell you they're struggling.

visual_keywords: woman standing alone in quiet room
example_scripts.script:
She stopped explaining herself.
Not because she stopped caring.
But because she finally understood—
the right people don't need the explanation.

The ones who love you
will believe your silence
before they question your reasons.

She wasn't difficult.
She was just done translating herself
for people who never wanted to understand.

Save this for anyone
who calls your standards too high.

example_scripts.topic: What They Never Say at Funerals
example_scripts.script:
At funerals, nobody talks about how busy they were.

Nobody says:
'I'm glad I missed that birthday.'
'I'm glad I sent a text instead of showing up.'

They talk about the last time they really laughed with someone.
The last time they really listened.

The time they had… that they thought they'd always have.

Don't wait for a funeral to remember what matters.
Send this to someone you keep meaning to call.

example_scripts.topic: The Morning After You Stop Trying
example_scripts.script:
The morning after you stop trying
feels like failure.

Until it doesn't.

Until you realize—
peace isn't something they withheld from you.
It was always yours.
You just kept leaving the room to go find them.

Stop leaving your own peace
for people who already left.

Save this for the days you forget that.

Sunday used to feel like the world agreed to pause.
Everyone gathered… not because they were summoned… but because they wanted to be there.
The table wasn't fancy… but every seat was filled.
Now Sundays come and go… and most people spend them alone with a screen.
We have more freedom than ever… and nowhere we truly want to be.
Send this to someone you used to spend Sundays with.

Remember when you could tell someone everything… without saying a word?
A glance across a room used to carry the weight of a whole conversation.
People knew how to look at each other… really look.
Now we stare at glass… and call it connection.
The saddest part… is we don't even notice what we've stopped seeing.
Tag someone who still knows how to really listen.

To be honest…
you're more than just my favorite person.

You're the thought I reach for
when the day gets too heavy.
The voice I want to hear
before I fall asleep.

I don't know how to explain it to anyone else.
I just know that everything feels quieter
when you're near.

Tag the person who makes the world feel smaller — in the best way. ❤️

You don't even know what you do.

You walk in
and my whole chest just… exhales.
Like something that was held tight
finally remembers how to let go.

I've been looking for that feeling my whole life.
Turns out it had your name.

Send this to the person who is your calm. ❤️

example_scripts.topic: The Wound That Becomes the Gift
example_scripts.script:
Rumi once wrote…
'The wound is the place
where the Light enters you.'

And quietly… that became everything.

Because we've been taught to hide the broken parts.
To patch them quickly. Apologize for the cracks.

But what if that's exactly where you let the light in?
What if the places that hurt you most
are the places that grew you deepest?

Save this for the days the wound feels like weakness.

example_scripts.topic: You Have Power Over Your Mind
example_scripts.script:
Marcus Aurelius wrote in his journal…
'You have power over your mind.
Not outside events.'

And quietly… that changed how I face everything.

You cannot control what people say about you.
What they choose. What they walk away from.

But the story you tell yourself about all of it?
That… is entirely yours.

Save this for the next time
the world feels like it's against you.
